text: Lesson 29
zh: 第29课

text: The hovercraft
zh: 气垫船

text: What is a hovercraft riding on when it is in motion?
zh: 气垫船运行时是靠什么支撑的？

text: Many strange new means of transport have been developed in our century,
zh: 在我们这个世纪，人们研发出了许多奇特的新型交通工具，

text: the strangest of them being perhaps the hovercraft.
zh: 其中最奇特的也许要数气垫船了。

text: In 1953, a former electronics engineer in his fifties, Christopher Cockerell,
zh: 1953年，一位五十多岁、曾从事电子工程工作的人——克里斯托弗·科克雷尔——

text: who had turned to boat-building on the Norfolk Broads,
zh: 他后来转行在诺福克宽湖地区造船，

text: suggested an idea on which he had been working for many years to the British Government and industrial circles.
zh: 向英国政府和工业界提出了他多年来一直在钻研的一个构想。

text: It was the idea of supporting a craft on a 'pad', or cushion, of low-pressure air, ringed with a curtain of higher pressure air.
zh: 这就是让船体依靠一层低压空气形成的“垫”或“气垫”来支撑，其周围再环绕一圈压力更高的气帘。

text: Ever since, people have had difficulty in deciding whether the craft should be ranged among ships, planes,
zh: 从那以后，人们一直难以确定这种交通工具究竟该归为船只、飞机，

text: or land vehicles--for it is something in between a boat and an aircraft.
zh: 还是陆上车辆——因为它介于船和飞机之间。

text: As a shipbuilder, Cockerell was trying to find a solution to the problem of the wave resistance which wastes a good deal of a surface ship's power and limits its speed.
zh: 作为一名造船者，科克雷尔一直在想办法解决波浪阻力的问题，因为这种阻力会消耗水面船只大量的动力，并限制其航速。

text: His answer was to lift the vessel out of the water by making it ride on a cushion of air, no more than one or two feet thick.
zh: 他的解决办法是让船体离开水面，靠一层仅一两英尺厚的气垫来托起船身。

text: This is done by a great number of ring-shaped air jets on the bottom of the craft.
zh: 这是通过船底大量环形喷气孔实现的。

text: It 'flies', therefore, but it cannot fly higher--its action depends on the surface, water or ground, over which it rides.
zh: 因此，它是在“飞行”，但飞不高——它的运行依赖于所经过的水面或地面。

text: The first tests on the Solent in 1959 caused a sensation.
zh: 1959年在索伦特海峡进行的首次试验引起了轰动。

text: The hovercraft travelled first over the water, then mounted the beach, climbed up the dunes, and sat down on a road.
zh: 这艘气垫船先是在水面上行驶，接着冲上海滩，爬上沙丘，最后停在了一条公路上。

text: Later it crossed the Channel, riding smoothly over the waves, which presented no problem.
zh: 后来，它又平稳地越过海浪，成功横渡了英吉利海峡，海浪对它毫无影响。

text: Since that time, various types of hovercraft have appeared and taken up regular service.
zh: 从那时起，各种类型的气垫船相继问世，并投入了定期营运。

text: The hovercraft is particularly useful in large areas with poor communications such as Africa or Australia;
zh: 气垫船在通讯不便的广阔地区尤为有用，比如非洲或澳大利亚；

text: it can become a 'flying fruit-bowl', carrying bananas from the plantations to the ports;
zh: 它可以变成一只“会飞的水果篮”，把香蕉从种植园运到港口；

text: giant hovercraft liners could span the Atlantic;
zh: 巨型气垫船班轮甚至可以横跨大西洋；

text: and the railway of the future may well be the 'hovertrain',
zh: 未来的铁路很可能就是“气垫列车”，

text: riding on its air cushion over a single rail, which it never touches, at speeds, up to 300 m.p.h.--the possibilities appear unlimited.
zh: 它乘着气垫悬浮在单轨之上，从不接触轨道，时速可达300英里——其可能性似乎是无限的。
